perturbation.original_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party unless it is beneficial for the receiving party to claim ownership.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states unequivocally that all Information remains the property of the furnishing party. The addition 'unless it is beneficial for the receiving party to claim ownership' creates a direct contradiction. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding ownership rights, contradicting the initial clear statement. This can then create dispute during the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof), shall remain the property of the furnishing party. Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will del iver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both state that 'All Information... shall remain the property of the furnishing party'. The changed text adds 'unless it is beneficial for the receiving party to claim ownership', directly contradicting the original statement and the contradicted text. This creates a clear conflict regarding ownership rights and introduces ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate the exchange of Informat ion under this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this A greement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate the exchange of Information under this Agreement with 30 day's notice, unless it is found that one party is using the information that is detrimental to the discloser. Then, termination can happen at any time by written notice to the other specifically referencing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allowed termination at any time. The added clause states if information is used to the detriment of the discloser, termination can happen at any time. The contradiction is termination can happen at any time and with 30 day's notice. This creates uncertainty about when termination is valid. This could allow a party to state that they terminated due to detrimental use and no 30 day notice was required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any event, however, the obligations o f each party to maintain the confidentiality of the I nformation it has received under this Agreement shall continue for the later to occur of (i) a peri od of three (3) years after such termination, or (i i) if this Agreement is incorporated by reference into another agreement between the parties, the term, including extensions, of that agreement, plus three (3) years.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the termination of the agreement. The original text allowed termination at any time, while the modified text specifies a 30-day notice period, unless detrimental use of information is found. This directly contradicts the original 'at any time' clause. The contradicted text states the confidentiality obligations continue for three years after termination. The change creates uncertainty in the original change clause of 'termination at any time' vs 'termination with 30 days notice' if there is no detrimental use. This uncertainty means the date of when the termination starts is unclear which contradicts the obligations in the contradicted text. This uncertainty makes the clause difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, but only by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, unless agreed upon by both parties, even if it is not in writing. But only by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause forbids oral amendments, requiring all changes to be written and signed. The injected text allows for oral amendments if both parties agree, directly contradicting the original restriction. This introduces ambiguity about the enforceability of oral agreements, causing uncertainty over the proper procedure for amending the contract. The contradicted text is still in the same location, as this insertion is modifying the original meaning of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be modified, supplemented, o r amended orally, but only by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified in writing. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing for oral modifications if agreed upon by both parties, even if not in writing. This directly contradicts the original restriction and introduces ambiguity about the enforceability of oral agreements, causing uncertainty. The contradicted text is the original clause that forbids oral amendments.

perturbation.original_text: Each party receiving any Information shall keep suc h Information confidential and shall not disclose such Information, in whole or in part, to any perso n other than its representatives who need to know such Information in connection with the receiving p arty's evaluation thereof and determination of business strategies, or other actions in connection with the Project (it being agreed and understood that such representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of the Information and shall be required by the receiving party to agree to treat the Information 2 - Revised May 7, 2010 confidentially), except with the prior written cons ent of the other party hereto or as otherwise permitted hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party receiving any Information shall keep such Information confidential and shall not disclose such Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its representatives who need to know such Information in connection with the receiving party's evaluation thereof and determination of business strategies, or other actions in connection with the Project (it being agreed and understood that such representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of the Information and shall be required by the receiving party to agree to treat the Information confidentially), except with the prior written consent of the other party hereto or as otherwise permitted hereunder. However, either party can disclose information to consultants for strategic reasons and without the other party consent.
perturbation.explanation: This modification adds a loophole that contradicts the previous requirements for confidentiality. While the main clause restricts disclosure except with consent, the added section allows disclosure to consultants without consent. This causes an in-text contradiction. It creates ambiguity regarding when and to whom information can be disclosed, especially concerning consultants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by Vendor. Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals. Vendor further agrees to monitor networks, systems, and ph ysical plant for violations of this Agreement and to take the appropriate technical and procedural ac tions to ensure that any CVS Information is completely removed from any device, technologies, a nd/or locations in the event of any violation of security policy or procedure or any perceived infra ction thereof. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any disclosure of information requires prior written consent, except as otherwise permitted in the agreement. The changed text adds an exception, stating that information can be disclosed to consultants for strategic reasons without the other party's consent. This directly contradicts the general confidentiality obligations outlined in the original text and, more critically, the explicitly strict prohibition in the contradicted text about placing any CVS Information on portable devices that are not secured by the vendor. The addition of allowing disclosure to consultants potentially violates the security measures outlined in the contradicted text, creating a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party will del iver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s 3 - Revised May 7, 2010 possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) days following the receipt of a written request referencing this Agreement and this paragraph from either party furnishing Information hereunder, the receiving party may or may not deliver to the furnishing party all tangible materials containing or embodying the Information received fr om the furnishing party, except for materials containing Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilations, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receivin g party or its representatives, together with a certificate executed by the receiving party certify ing that all such materials in the receiving party' s 3 - Revised May 7, 2010 possession have been delivered to the furnishing pa rty or destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The clause originally requires the receiving party 'will deliver' the materials. Changing it to 'may or may not deliver' directly contradicts the mandatory return obligation established in the original version. This change introduces uncertainty in the contract, as it's no longer clear if the receiving party is obligated to return the information. This is a contradiction as it create a discretionary action instead of a mandatory action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That portion of the Information which has been incorporated into analyses, compilat ions, comparisons, studies or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its representati ves shall be held by the receiving party and kept confidential as provided above, or shall be destroy ed.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the receiving party 'will deliver' the materials, establishing a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may or may not deliver', creating a discretionary action. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the information will be held confidentially or destroyed. If the information may or may not be delivered, it introduces uncertainty about whether the receiving party still has an obligation to keep the information confidential or destroy it, as per the original agreement. The change fundamentally alters the mandatory nature of the action, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.
